🌿 Evergreen Multi-Engine Sourcing System
Improve any engine without redeployment:
• Airtable: exa_queries, competitor_anchors, sectors, naf_codes
• engine_config.json: RSS feeds, Google News templates, caps
• backfill_engine.py: VC portfolio URLs
• New MATCHes auto-seeded into Exa Similar for next cycle.

A. WEEKLY SOURCING MACHINE — Full Calendar

Exa Semantic Search — Solutions for Business Travel
↻ 7-day cycle · 1 slot/day ○ IDLE

Exa neural semantic search using category='company' → finds startup homepages matching your “Here is a…” query. No date filter: category='company' does not support freshness. Deduplication against Airtable handles freshness (seen URLs/names are skipped).

Rotation: prod=2/day per thesis, test=1/day per thesis cycling through 28 total | Results: 5/query test, 25/query prod | Cost: $0.008 test / $0.046 prod
THIS RUN — 1 query for Solutions for Business Travel (test mode: 1 of 2 prod queries):
1. regional_hotel_platforms“Here is a growth-stage hotel platform with 1-10M EUR ARR in Portugal.”
ⓘ No date filter — Exa category='company' does not support freshness filtering. Deduplication against Airtable handles freshness.

🔧 HOW TO ENHANCE THIS ENGINE
Add queries: open Airtable → your fund_thesis → each angle's exa_queries. Write “Here is a company that [does X for Y customers]” describing a company you'd invest in. Best queries are specific about the customer type and problem (e.g. “Here is a B2B SaaS startup helping hotel chains automate their RFP process”). Runs 2/day prod (1/day test), rotating through all queries over N days.
Exa Similar (Lookalike) — Solutions for Business Travel
↻ 7-day cycle · 1 slot/day ○ IDLE

Exa find_similar() → sends competitor/MATCH homepage URLs and returns companies with similar website content based on neural embedding similarity. Does not support category='company' so some non-company pages may appear (filtered by gate). Competitor domains are automatically excluded from results.

Seeds: 40 competitor anchors + 0 MATCH URLs = 40 total | Rotation: prod=5/day, test=2/day | Cost: $0.016 test / $0.065 prod
THIS RUN — 2 seeds for Solutions for Business Travel (test mode: 2 of 5 prod seeds):
1. MTrip (anchor)
2. International SOS (anchor)
ⓘ No date filter — find_similar does not support freshness. Deduplication handles freshness. MATCHes become seeds in future runs.

🔧 HOW TO ENHANCE THIS ENGINE
Add seeds: open Airtable → your fund_thesis → each angle's competitor_anchors. Add exact company names (e.g. “Harvey”, “Ironclad”). Each name is converted to https://www.{name}.com and used as a seed URL. Pick direct competitors in your target niche — broad platforms (SAP, Salesforce) produce too many irrelevant lookalikes. Every MATCH you approve in Airtable auto-becomes a seed in future runs.
Google News RSS — Solutions for Business Travel
↻ Daily (all items) ✓ ACTIVE — 40 articles

Fetches fresh Google News RSS with after:yesterday filter — always the last 24h. Gemini 2.5 Flash reads each article and extracts company names mentioned. Runs ALL queries every day (no rotation): freshness comes from the after:yesterday filter.

Rotation: NONE — all queries daily | Cost: $0.00 (free Google RSS) | Test cap: 40 articles, Prod: 150
Fetched: 40 articles → Gemini miner → 8 companies → evaluated: 7 for Solutions for Business Travel

Loch0NO MATCHSTEP 1: Loch is a platform for thoughtful writing and organizing ideas, which does not serve the corporate business travel industry as its primary vertical.https://www.lochapp.com/news.google.com/rss/articles/CBMie0FVX3lxTFBB
Aidence0NO MATCHSTEP 1 fails. The company develops AI solutions for lung cancer detection, which is in the medical/healthcare sector, not the corporate business travel industry.https://www.aidence.com/about-us/news.google.com/rss/articles/CBMie0FVX3lxTFBB
DeepSea Technologies0NO MATCHSTEP 1: The company's core product serves the maritime industry for vessel efficiency, not the corporate business travel industry.https://www.deepsea.ai/companynews.google.com/rss/articles/CBMie0FVX3lxTFBB
Insempra0NO MATCHSTEP 1 (vertical gate) fails. This company is a biotechnology platform focused on sustainable ingredients, which does not serve the corporate business travel industry as its primary vertical. It falls under the 'general ESG' reject criteria for the sustainability angle, and is unrelated to other travel-specific angles.https://www.startupintros.com/orgs/insempranews.google.com/rss/articles/CBMie0FVX3lxTFBB
Hume AI0NO MATCHSTEP 1 fails because Hume AI focuses on 'emotion AI' and 'affective computing,' which are not part of the corporate business travel industry as its primary vertical. It does not provide services such as hotel aggregation, ERP integration, payment processing, or sustainability compliance within travel.https://www.hume.ai/contactnews.google.com/rss/articles/CBMie0FVX3lxTFBB
Quantilope0NO MATCHSTEP 1: The company provides an AI-powered consumer intelligence platform for market research insights, which is not within the corporate business travel industry.https://www.quantilope.com/aboutnews.google.com/rss/articles/CBMie0FVX3lxTFBB
Montonio10NO MATCHSTEP 1 fails. Montonio is a general fintech company providing payment solutions, not specifically focused on the corporate business travel industry. It falls under the 'general fintech' reject criterion.https://ee.linkedin.com/company/montoniostartup Europe raises seed
GuruSup—DROPPEDAlready in Airtable: Deduped — table: profund_sourcing, base: appC8XYbrR4uWsuZchttps://github.com/gurusupstatic
🔧 HOW TO ENHANCE THIS ENGINE
Add queries: open Airtable → your fund_thesis → each angle's google_alert_queries. Best formats: "[sector keyword] startup funding", "[company type] raises Series A", "[technology] B2B SaaS". Each query generates a Google News RSS URL with after:yesterday. Runs daily at no cost.

🔧 HOW TO ENHANCE THIS ENGINE
Add queries: open Airtable → each angle's apify_linkedin_posts_queries. Each phrase targets a different signal type. Best patterns: hiring signal (“hiring VP Sales [sector]”), funding signal (“raised seed [sector]”), product launch (“just launched [product type]”). 1 query runs per day per thesis — the full pool rotates over N days.
LinkedIn Jobs — Solutions for Business Travel
↻ 7-day cycle · 1 slot/day ⚠ API KEY MISSING

Apify LinkedIn Jobs → job postings from the last 24h. scrapeCompany=true returns the company website URL directly. Filter f_CS=1,2 restricts to 1–50 employee companies. A startup actively hiring CFO, VP Sales, or engineers in your niche = strong growth signal. 1 entry/day per thesis with weekly angle rotation.

Per-thesis | 1 entry/day from 28 total · 4 angles rotating weekly | f_TPR=r86400 (24h) | f_CS=1,2 (1–50 empl.) | sortBy=DD | Cost: ~$0.03–$0.08/run

🔧 HOW TO ENHANCE THIS ENGINE
Add entries: open Airtable → apify_linkedin_jobs_calendar for your fund_thesis angle. Each entry targets 1 day of the week: keywords (e.g. “CFO hotel technology”) + city (e.g. “Paris”). Best signals: C-level hires (CFO, CTO, VP Sales) = fundraising/growth in progress. Rotate cities across EU hubs each day (Paris, London, Berlin, Amsterdam, Madrid, Stockholm, Zurich). f_CS=1,2 keeps companies 1–50 employees. f_TPR=r86400 = last 24h only.
Fundraising — EU Funding News (Solutions for Business Travel)
↻ Daily (all items) ✓ ACTIVE — 3 leads

Fundraising scraper (Apify). Scrapes latest EU funding news from TechCrunch, Crunchbase News, FinSMEs. ⚠ We cannot target a specific sector — the actor always fetches the latest news. Today-only filter applied post-fetch. All results then evaluated by the LLM (which is the real sector filter).

Shared engine (same results for all theses) | Rotation: daily — same empty payload every run | Cost: ~$0.05–$0.20/run | Test: 10 leads, Prod: 30 leads

🔧 HOW TO ENHANCE THIS ENGINE
This engine scrapes TechCrunch, Crunchbase News, and FinSMEs automatically — no sector targeting is possible. The LLM evaluation step is the real filter. To improve precision, refine the thesis gate questions in your fund_thesis.

↻ 7-day cycle · 1 slot/day ⚠ API KEY MISSING

Fix: modal secret create apify-token APIFY_TOKEN=xxx
🔧 HOW TO ENHANCE THIS ENGINE
Add entries: open Airtable → apify_crunchbase_calendar for your fund_thesis angle. Each entry: exact Crunchbase industry enum + city + employee band. Use ultra-specific enums (e.g. “Hotel”, “Legal Tech”, “Electronic Health Record (EHR)”) — broad enums like “Travel” or “Financial Technology” return thousands of irrelevant results. Rotate cities across EU hubs each day (Paris, London, Berlin, Amsterdam, Madrid, Stockholm, Zurich). Country is auto-filled from the city — never add a separate country field.
RSS — EU Startup Media (Shared)
↻ Daily (all items) ✓ ACTIVE — 50 leads

RSS feeds from EU startup media (Sifted, TechCrunch EU, Maddyness, etc.) → Gemini 2.5 Flash reads each article and extracts company names. Runs ALL feeds every day (cap: 50 articles test, 500 prod). Shared across all theses.

Shared (evaluated per-thesis) | Rotation: NONE — all feeds daily (cap limits) | Test cap: 50 articles | Prod cap: 500 articles
⚠ Known: some sites obfuscate company names → garbled names filtered before LLM evaluation

🔧 HOW TO ENHANCE THIS ENGINE
Add feeds: open execution/config/engine_config.json, find rss_feeds, and append any RSS URL from EU startup/VC media. No Airtable change needed. Raise article_cap to fetch more per run (test=50, prod=500). To improve precision, add media sources focused on your specific sector (e.g. healthtech newsletters, legaltech blogs).
Backfill — VC Portfolios & Directories (Shared)
↻ 7-day cycle · 1 slot/day ✓ ACTIVE — 634 leads

Firecrawl + Gemini 2.5 Flash → scrapes VC/accelerator portfolio pages and extracts portfolio companies. Rotates through all sources (~9/day). Gemini reads the raw HTML and returns a JSON list of {name, url, description}.

🔧 HOW TO ENHANCE THIS ENGINE
Add a VC firm: find their portfolio page URL, add it to BACKFILL_SOURCES in execution/sourcing_v3/engines/backfill_engine.py. Format: "Firm Name": "https://firm.com/portfolio". It auto-rotates into the weekly cycle (total_sources ÷ 7 per day). Test mode scrapes only 2 sources/run to limit Firecrawl costs. Cost: $0.00533/page Firecrawl + ~$0.001 Gemini Flash per page.
Pappers FR — Company Registry (Shared)
↻ Daily (all items) ○ IDLE

French company registry (INPI via Pappers API) → finds recently registered companies by NAF code. 90-day lookback by default. Only covers French companies — useful if your thesis includes French-founded B2B SaaS.

Shared | Rotation: NONE — all NAF codes daily (90-day lookback) | Cost: Pappers subscription
ACTIVE NAF CODES (2, all run daily):
CodeSector
6201ZCustom software development
6202A—

[06:32:48] [Pappers FR] API error 401 for NAF 6201Z: {"statusCode":401,"error":"Vous n'avez plus assez de crédits pour effectuer cette requête","message":"Vous pouvez prendre un abonnement supérieur ou acheter des Pay as you go depuis votre espace membre Pappers."}
[06:32:48] [Pappers FR] API error 401 for NAF 6202A: {"statusCode":401,"error":"Vous n'avez plus assez de crédits pour effectuer cette requête","message":"Vous pouvez prendre un abonnement supérieur ou acheter des Pay as you go depuis votre espace membre Pappers."}
[06:32:48] [Pappers] 0 companies from French registry
🔧 HOW TO ENHANCE THIS ENGINE
Add codes: append NAF codes to naf_codes in Airtable shared config (under sourcing_config.shared). Find relevant codes at pappers.fr/code-naf. To change the lookback window, edit pappers_config.lookback_days in engine_config.json (default: 90 days). Current codes are listed above.
GitHub — Developer Repos (Shared)
↻ Daily (all items) ○ IDLE

Searches GitHub repos and organizations for projects matching merged sector keywords. Finds technical founders building in your space before they raise. Free GitHub API, runs daily.

Shared | Rotation: NONE | Cost: $0.00 (free) | Test: 30 leads | Prod: 30 leads

🔧 HOW TO ENHANCE THIS ENGINE
Add sectors: edit sectors_plain in each angle in Airtable — merged across all theses. GitHub searches repos/orgs; HN searches Show HN posts. Both run daily at no cost. Keep keywords short and precise (e.g. “hotel-rfp”, “legal-ai”, “health-compliance”) — specific technical terms that a founder would use when naming a project.

CRT.sh — Certificate Transparency (Shared)
↻ Daily (all items) ○ IDLE

Monitors Certificate Transparency logs (crt.sh) for new domain registrations matching sector keywords. A new domain with “legalai”, “hoteltech” etc. in the name = early signal of a new startup. Free, runs daily (test: 3 keywords, prod: 10 keywords).

Shared | Rotation: NONE — daily | Cost: $0.00 (free crt.sh) | Prod cap: 50 leads

🔧 HOW TO ENHANCE THIS ENGINE
Add sector keywords: edit cert_transparency_keywords in engine_config.json. Use compound words founders typically include in domain names (e.g. “legalai”, “hotelops”, “medtech”, “travelops”). Avoid generic terms like “software” or “tech” — too many false positives. Runs automatically in prod every day.
